skill.name: azure-mgmt-apicenter-py
skill.description: Guidance and Python examples for using Azure API Center Management SDK to manage API inventory, versions, definitions, deployments, environments, and metadata.
skill.summary: Manage Azure API Center resources with Python SDK examples.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment access, weak cryptography, and a critical combination heuristic. Review found these are Markdown installation commands and Azure SDK examples, not executable skill logic or credential exfiltration. No prompt injection text or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misidentified as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to fenced Markdown examples, including pip installation and API operation snippets. The skill file does not execute these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported lines are documentation fences, table entries, or sample code. There is no script wrapper, shell invocation, or hidden execution path in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URLs in Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are sample API metadata values for terms of service, Azure portal, and runtime server fields. They are not used for covert outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear inside Azure API Center resource examples. No code sends local files, environment variables, or credentials to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Subscription Environment Variable Used for SDK Configuration
low_findings.description: The environment variable access reads AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID to configure ApiCenterMgmtClient. This is expected for Azure SDK authentication examples and is not exfiltrated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Only a subscription identifier is read, and it is passed to the Azure management client. No evidence shows secret harvesting or transmission to an unrelated endpoint.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches occur in ordinary words or API description fields, and the reconnaissance alert is an environment variable placeholder. No cryptographic code or host probing is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain YAML prose, sample descriptions, or Azure metadata fields. They do not call weak hash algorithms, encryption APIs, or system discovery commands.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The combined pattern is caused by installation instructions, Azure client configuration, and sample URL fields appearing in one Markdown document. The semantic context does not indicate malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The document contains legitimate Azure SDK usage examples, but users may run snippets against live Azure resources. Human review should treat this as low operational risk, not malware.

content.user_title: Manage Azure API Center with Python
content.value_statement: Azure API Center users need repeatable examples for registering and governing APIs. This skill provides focused Python SDK guidance for API inventory, versions, definitions, deployments, environments, and metadata.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install azure-mgmt-apicenter and azure-identity packages.
Provides examples for creating and listing Azure API Center services.
Shows API registration, version creation, and API definition import flows.
Covers environment, deployment, and metadata schema management examples.
Summarizes supported operation groups in the management client.
limitations: Does not create resources unless the user runs the examples in their environment.
Requires valid Azure credentials, subscription access, and installed Python packages.
Examples use placeholder resource names and must be adapted before production use.
Does not cover every Azure API Center model or long-running operation option.

use_cases.title: Set Up API Inventory
use_cases.description: Create an Azure API Center and register APIs so teams can manage ownership, lifecycle stage, and catalog metadata.

use_cases.title: Track API Deployments
use_cases.description: Model environments and deployments to record where API versions are running and which definitions they use.

use_cases.title: Standardize Governance Metadata
use_cases.description: Define metadata schemas for consistent classification, ownership, and review processes across an API portfolio.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to authenticate with Azure API Center Management SDK for Python using DefaultAzureCredential and my subscription ID.

prompt_templates.title: Register an API
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me register a REST API in Azure API Center with title, lifecycle stage, contact, and terms of service metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Model Deployments
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Python workflow that defines a production environment and links an API version definition to an active deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Design Governance Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan metadata schemas for API classification, ownership, and maturity in Azure API Center, then show the Python SDK calls to create them.

output_examples.input: I need to create an API Center and add a REST API.
output_examples.output: The response outlines package installation, Azure authentication, service creation, and API registration steps with placeholders for resource group, service, workspace, and contact values.

output_examples.input: I want to document where a production API is deployed.
output_examples.output: The response explains how to create a production environment, reference the API definition, and record an active deployment with a runtime endpoint.

output_examples.input: I need consistent governance metadata for all APIs.
output_examples.output: The response recommends metadata fields such as data classification and ownership, then describes how to create matching metadata schemas.

best_practices: Replace all placeholder resource names, URLs, and contacts before running examples.
Use least-privilege Azure roles for accounts that manage API Center resources.
Validate API definitions and metadata schemas in a test subscription first.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production subscription identifiers or tenant details into shared prompts.
Do not run create or update examples without confirming the target resource group and workspace.
Do not use sample URLs or contact addresses as production API metadata.

faq.question: Does this skill manage Azure API Center directly?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and examples. Actions occur only if the user runs the Python SDK code with valid Azure credentials.

faq.question: Which Python package does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on azure-mgmt-apicenter and uses azure-identity for Azure authentication examples.

faq.question: Can it import OpenAPI definitions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an example for importing an inline OpenAPI specification into an API definition.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: The skill file itself does not make requests. The Azure SDK examples contact Azure services when a user runs them.

faq.question: Is it safe for production environments?
faq.answer: Use it as a reference. Review permissions, resource names, lifecycle stages, and metadata before running commands in production.

faq.question: Does it include prompt injection text?
faq.answer: No evidence of prompt injection language was found during review of SKILL.md.
